landing.badge: Pakistan Edition — Lahore, Karachi, Islamabad & 50+ cities
landing.subtitle: HealthPilot AI is your free healthcare navigator for Pakistan — describe symptoms in English or Urdu, get AI-guided specialty advice, then find real doctors and hospitals near you. No account needed to start.
landing.trustLine: Private chat · Bilingual · Guidance only — not a diagnosis
landing.whatIsP1: HealthPilot AI connects three things people in Pakistan usually search separately: understanding symptoms, choosing the right type of doctor, and finding someone nearby you can actually visit or call.
landing.whatIsP2: Start with our AI symptom checker for specialty and urgency guidance. Then open the nationwide doctor directory (7,000+ listings), live hospital map, health guides, and WHO Pakistan statistics — all in one app.
landing.does1: Suggest which specialist fits your symptoms (GP, cardiologist, ENT, etc.)
landing.does2: Flag emergencies early and show safety tips
landing.does3: Find doctors by city, specialty, fee, and GPS distance
landing.does4: Show hospitals & clinics on a live map
landing.symptomSpotlightTitle: AI Symptom Checker — how it helps
landing.symptomSpotlightSubtitle: A short, private conversation that matches your story to the right kind of care in Pakistan.
landing.symptomSpotlight1Text: Type in English or Urdu — fever, allergies, palpitations, pain, duration, and what makes it worse.
landing.symptomSpotlight2Text: The AI asks focused questions (not a long form) to understand urgency and the right specialty.
landing.symptomSpotlight3Text: Get guidance, first-aid tips, red flags, and a filtered list of matching doctors and facilities near you.
landing.examplesSubtitle: Every journey starts free — pick the path that fits you.
landing.example1Desc: Sneezing, itchy eyes, dust outdoors → ENT or GP guidance with antihistamine tips and nearby specialists.
landing.example2Desc: Racing heart after tea or exams → Cardiologist or GP routing — not the wrong specialty.
landing.example3Desc: Search 7,000+ doctors directly by city, specialty, consultation fee, gender, and Near Me distance.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: From symptoms to booking — a clear path built for how people in Pakistan actually seek care.
landing.whyUsSubtitle: Built for Pakistan: bilingual tools, real doctor listings, live facility maps, and official health data.
landing.quickStart: Explore the platform — no sign-up required
landing.actionSymptomsDesc: Private chat in English or Urdu. Specialty guidance, urgency level, NHS-informed references, and emergency alerts.
landing.actionDoctorsDesc: 7,000+ doctors — search by city, specialty, fee, gender, and Near Me (GPS distance).
landing.actionFacilitiesDesc: Live hospitals and clinics near you from OpenStreetMap, with map view.
landing.actionHealthDesc: Plain-language guides on dengue, diabetes, hypertension, and more.
landing.readySubtitle: Open any tool below and use it immediately. Create an account only if you want to save appointments.
landing.step1Text: Use the symptom checker in English or Urdu — pain, fever, duration, and other details in a simple conversation.
landing.step2Text: Get a recommended medical specialty, urgency level, and safety tips. Emergency symptoms are flagged immediately. This is guidance only, not a diagnosis.
landing.step3Text: Open the doctor directory with 7,000+ listings. Filter by city, specialty, consultation fee, hospital, and Near Me to see who is closest.
landing.step4Text: View profiles with clinic location, fees, languages, PMDC number where available, and verification badges. Check hospitals on the map if you need a facility.
landing.step5Text: Book Marham doctors through their official online appointment page. For others, call the clinic or use WhatsApp inquiry — guest-friendly, no forced sign-up.
landing.feature1Desc: Conversational checker with bilingual support, specialty routing, medical reference grounding, and instant emergency triage before AI responds.
landing.feature2Desc: Nationwide listings normalized from Marham — search Lahore, Karachi, Islamabad, Rawalpindi, Multan, Peshawar, and dozens more cities.
landing.feature3Desc: Sort doctors by GPS distance in your area, or filter by specialty, fee range, female/male doctor, language, and hospital name.
landing.feature4Desc: Hospitals and clinics near you from OpenStreetMap — always fresh map data alongside the doctor directory.
landing.feature5Desc: Official World Health Organization data made readable — life expectancy, leading causes of death, and national health indicators.
landing.feature6Desc: PMDC numbers and verification badges where available. Clear disclaimers: AI is informational; always consult a qualified clinician.
landing.whoDesc: HealthPilot includes a dedicated Health Statistics page powered by the World Health Organization Global Health Observatory — life expectancy, population health, and leading causes of illness explained in simple language.
landing.whoBullet1: Official WHO open data for Pakistan
landing.whoBullet2: Charts and tips you can understand
landing.whoSource: Source: World Health Organization (WHO), Global Health Observatory
landing.medicalDisclaimer: HealthPilot AI provides health information and doctor discovery only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. In an emergency, call Rescue 1122 or go to the nearest hospital.

symptoms.title: Symptom Checker
symptoms.pageBadge: AI health guide · Not a diagnosis
symptoms.pageSubtitle: Describe your symptoms in a short conversation. We'll suggest the right medical specialty, urgency level, and doctors near you in Pakistan.
symptoms.welcomeTip1: Mention how long you've had symptoms and how severe they are.
symptoms.welcomeTip2: Answer follow-up questions — they help narrow down the right specialist.
symptoms.welcomeTip3: Tap an example below or type in your own words (English or Urdu).
symptoms.stagedHint: Final assessment after a few more details
symptoms.sendHint: Press Enter to send · Shift+Enter for a new line
symptoms.subtitleEn: Describe how you feel — AI will recommend the right specialist
symptoms.subtitleUr: اپنی تکلیف بیان کریں — AI آپ کو صحیح ڈاکٹر تک پہنچائے گا
symptoms.disclaimer: AI analysis is not a medical diagnosis. Always consult a doctor.
symptoms.placeholderEn: Describe how you feel... (e.g. fever and headache for 2 days)
symptoms.resultsSubtitle: AI-suggested guidance — not a medical diagnosis
symptoms.ragSourcesHint: Relevant excerpts from NHS UK and Pakistan health guidelines that informed this guidance.
symptoms.emergencyText: Call Rescue 1122, Edhi 115, or go to the nearest ER immediately.
symptoms.chatSubtitle: Chat with AI — I'll ask follow-up questions for a better assessment
symptoms.chatPlaceholderEn: Type your symptoms... (e.g. fever for 2 days)

health.title: Health Awareness — Pakistan
health.subtitle: Common health conditions in Pakistan and when to seek care
health.dengueDesc: Seasonal in Pakistan (monsoon). Symptoms: high fever, body pain, rash. Seek care if bleeding or severe weakness.
health.typhoidDesc: Water-borne, common in Pakistan. Symptoms: prolonged fever, stomach pain, weakness. Get a blood test.
health.hepatitisDesc: High prevalence in Pakistan. Get screened if you have fatigue, jaundice, or abdominal pain.
health.diabetesDesc: Rapidly increasing in Pakistan. Watch for thirst, frequent urination, blurred vision, and slow healing.
health.hypertensionDesc: Often silent. Regular BP checks recommended, especially after age 30.
health.malariaDesc: Endemic in some regions. Symptoms: fever with chills, sweating. Common in rural KPK and Balochistan.

whoStats.whoDataNote: Official open data — life expectancy, mortality, and disease burden. HealthPilot explains it in plain language; WHO publishes the numbers.
whoStats.title: Understand health in Pakistan
whoStats.subtitle: Clear, friendly facts from WHO to help you and your family stay informed — not a personal diagnosis.
whoStats.heroCardText: We turn official WHO health data into simple language you can actually use. Numbers are about the whole country — not a diagnosis for you.
whoStats.sectionKnowSubtitle: Two big picture facts every Pakistani should know.
whoStats.causesSectionTitle: What affects health most in Pakistan?
whoStats.causesSectionSubtitle: WHO’s latest national ranking (2021) — deaths per 100,000 people, plus warning signs to watch for.
whoStats.tipsSubtitle: Simple steps you can take today.
whoStats.bannerText: Our AI symptom checker helps you decide when to rest at home and when to see a doctor — in English or Urdu.
whoStats.loadingHint: Fetching the latest official figures for Pakistan. This may take a moment the first time.
whoStats.dataNote: Figures from World Health Organization · last updated {{date}}
whoStats.highlightLifeNote: How long a baby born in Pakistan might live (WHO estimate, {{year}})
whoStats.glanceSubtitle: What these numbers mean for families in Pakistan
whoStats.focusSubtitle: Why check-ups matter — especially for fever, cough, and children
whoStats.chartDonutDesc: How the top 5 causes compare to each other — not your personal risk.
whoStats.chartShareNote: Percentages show share among these top 5 only (WHO Global Health Estimates, 2021).
whoStats.causesSubtitle: Compared to each other — not your personal risk. Longer bar = more common as a cause of death in Pakistan.
whoStats.tip1: If you have fever, cough, or weakness for more than 2–3 days — see a doctor. In Pakistan, dengue, typhoid, and flu are common.
whoStats.tip2: Children under 5 need quick care for high fever, not drinking, or breathing difficulty.
whoStats.tip3: Heart disease and diabetes are very common — adults over 30 should check blood pressure and sugar yearly.
whoStats.tip4: This page is for awareness only. It is not a diagnosis. Use our symptom checker or visit a clinic for personal advice.
whoStats.disclaimer: This is general information about Pakistan as a country. It does not tell you what is wrong with you. Always talk to a qualified doctor for diagnosis and treatment.
whoStats.sourceLine: Source: World Health Organization (WHO), Global Health Observatory. Used under open data terms.
whoStats.whoProfile: See more on WHO Pakistan page

life_expectancy.title: Average lifespan
life_expectancy.explain: On average, people in Pakistan live about {{value}} years (WHO, {{year}}). A healthy lifestyle and regular check-ups can help.

population.title: Population
population.explain: Around {{value}} people live in Pakistan ({{year}}). A large and growing country with diverse health needs.

maternal_mortality.title: Mothers dying in childbirth
maternal_mortality.explain: About {{value}} mothers die per 100,000 births ({{year}}). Pregnant women should get regular antenatal care at a hospital or clinic.

under_five_mortality.title: Children dying before age 5
under_five_mortality.explain: About {{value}} children per 1,000 births do not reach age 5 ({{year}}). Vaccines, nutrition, and quick treatment for fever save lives.

tb_incidence.title: TB (tuberculosis)
tb_incidence.explain: Roughly {{value}} new TB cases per 100,000 people each year ({{year}}). Long cough, weight loss, or night sweats — get a chest check.

malaria_incidence.title: Malaria
malaria_incidence.explain: Malaria risk is about {{value}} per 1,000 people in at-risk areas ({{year}}). More common in some rural regions — use nets and seek care for fever with chills.

ncd_premature_risk.title: Risk of dying early from heart, sugar, or lung disease
ncd_premature_risk.explain: About {{value}}% chance between ages 30–70 from major long-term illnesses ({{year}}). Diet, exercise, and BP/sugar checks reduce risk.

health_expenditure_gdp.title: Money spent on health (country total)
health_expenditure_gdp.explain: Pakistan spends about {{value}}% of national income on healthcare ({{year}}). Public hospitals and clinics remain important for most families.

causeTip.ischaemic_heart: Chest pain, breathlessness, or arm pain — go to hospital urgently.
causeTip.covid_19: High fever, breathing difficulty, low oxygen — seek care; vaccines reduce severe illness.
causeTip.stroke: Sudden weakness, slurred speech, or face drooping — call Rescue 1122 immediately.
causeTip.prematurity: Pregnant women: regular antenatal visits; preterm babies need hospital NICU care.
causeTip.birth_asphyxia: Baby not breathing well at birth needs immediate hospital care.
causeTip.copd: Long-term cough, smoker’s breathlessness — see a chest specialist.
causeTip.lower_respiratory: High fever with cough or chest pain — doctor visit, especially in children.
causeTip.diabetes: Thirst, frequent urination, blurred vision — get a blood sugar test.
causeTip.tuberculosis: Cough over 2 weeks, weight loss — free TB test at hospitals.
causeTip.liver_cirrhosis: Yellow eyes, swelling, or long-term hepatitis — see a physician.
causeTip.tb_deaths: Cough over 2 weeks, weight loss — TB test at any hospital.
causeTip.road_injury: Wear seatbelts and helmets; go to ER after any serious accident.
causeTip.breast_cancer: Lumps or changes in the breast — doctor examination and screening.

dashboard.subtitle: Your personal healthcare dashboard
dashboard.checkSymptomsDesc: Describe your symptoms and get AI-powered specialty recommendations.
dashboard.noSessions: No symptom sessions yet. Try the symptom checker.
dashboard.checkupGeneralDesc: Visit a general physician for a routine wellness exam.
dashboard.checkupDiabetesDesc: Recommended for adults 30+ in Pakistan due to rising diabetes rates.
dashboard.checkupGynDesc: Important for women's health — annual screening recommended.
dashboard.checkupCardiacDesc: Blood pressure and cardiac check recommended for adults 45+.
dashboard.appointmentsDesc: Manage your upcoming and past visits
dashboard.noReviewsYet: No reviews yet. Be the first after your visit!

footer.tagline: AI-powered healthcare navigation for Pakistan. Find the right doctor based on your symptoms.
footer.disclaimer: HealthPilot AI provides AI-suggested guidance only and is not a substitute for professional medical diagnosis or treatment. Always consult a qualified doctor.
footer.disclaimerShort: AI guidance only — not a substitute for professional medical care. Always consult a qualified doctor.
